title: Joseph Shine v. Union of India
snippet: Section 497 of the Indian Penal Code is unconstitutional as it treats a woman as the property of her husband and denies her sexual autonomy and dignity.

title: K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India
snippet: The right to privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21 of the Constitution.

title: Rambabu Singh Thakur v. Sunil Arora
snippet: Political parties must publish the criminal antecedents of candidates on their websites and in newspapers to ensure informed voter choice.

title: State of Gujarat v. Kirtibhai Patel
snippet: Bail cannot be denied merely on the ground of the seriousness of the offence when the investigation is complete and the charge sheet has been filed.

title: Shreya Singhal v. Union of India
snippet: Section 66A of the Information Technology Act is struck down as it is vague, over-broad, and has a chilling effect on the freedom of speech and expression.

title: Lalita Kumari v. Government of Uttar Pradesh
snippet: Registration of an FIR is mandatory under Section 154 of the Code of Criminal Procedure if the information discloses a cognizable offence.

title: Arnesh Kumar v. State of Bihar
snippet: Police officers must not automatically arrest an accused in cases punishable with imprisonment of less than seven years without recording reasons.

title: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation v. Nilesh Shah
snippet: Encroachment on public land does not automatically entitle the occupant to compensation unless a valid title or long-standing possession is established.

title: State of Maharashtra v. Christian Community Welfare Council
snippet: Arrest of women should ordinarily be made in the presence of a lady constable and not after sunset or before sunrise except in exceptional circumstances.

title: Satender Kumar Antil v. Central Bureau of Investigation
snippet: Courts must categorize offences to streamline the grant of bail and reduce unnecessary pre-trial incarceration of undertrial prisoners.

title: Amit Sahni v. Commissioner of Police
snippet: The right to protest cannot be exercised by indefinitely occupying public spaces that cause inconvenience to other citizens' fundamental rights.

title: Patel Textiles v. State of Gujarat
snippet: A show-cause notice under the relevant taxation statute must clearly specify the grounds to allow the assessee a meaningful opportunity to respond.

title: Independent Thought v. Union of India
snippet: Sexual intercourse with a wife below eighteen years of age constitutes rape regardless of the exception for marital relations under Section 375 IPC.

title: Common Cause v. Union of India
snippet: Illegal mining operations that bypass statutory clearances cause irreversible harm to the environment and must attract restorative compensation.

title: Shakti Vahini v. Union of India
snippet: Khap panchayats or any group of individuals cannot take law into their own hands to prevent or penalize consensual marriages between adults.
